title: Feistel round template

holes.name: ROUND_KEY_TYPE
holes.description: Type of one round key, e.g. [48]

holes.name: HALF_TYPE
holes.description: Type of one state half, e.g. [32]

template_code:
round : (ROUND_KEY_TYPE, [2]HALF_TYPE) -> [2]HALF_TYPE
round (k, [l, r]) = [r, l ^ F_FUNC (r, k)]
usage_notes: Use when modeling Feistel-family ciphers such as DES-like designs.
Switch to concatenation form `r # (...)` if the surrounding code expects a flat block instead of `[2]HALF_TYPE`.
Usually paired with a final half-swap before the final permutation.
retrieval_text: Template for a Feistel round. Takes a round key and two state halves, returns swapped halves with XOR against a round function output.

title: Apply a normalized permutation table with @@
template_code:
permute : INPUT_TYPE -> OUTPUT_TYPE
permute xs = xs @@ TABLE_NAME
usage_notes: Normalize specification tables to zero-based indices before using `@@`.
Works for reordering, expansion, compression, and selection tables.
Best paired with a nearby constant-table entry so the table itself is also retrievable.
retrieval_text: Template for applying a normalized permutation table in Cryptol: xs @@ TABLE_NAME. Suitable for DES/AES-style reordering, expansion, or compression steps.

title: Convert 1-based specification indices to 0-based Cryptol indices
template_code:
zeroBasify xs = [ i - 1 | i <- xs ]

NORMALIZED_NAME = zeroBasify TABLE_NAME
usage_notes: Very common when porting textbook or standards permutation tables into Cryptol.
Use the normalized table only for Cryptol indexing operators like `@`, `@@`, `!`, or `!!`.
Keep the original table as a separate artifact if traceability back to the standard matters.
retrieval_text: Template for converting 1-based specification tables to 0-based Cryptol index tables using a comprehension that subtracts one from each entry.

title: Scheduled rotations plus compression-selection key schedule

holes.name: ROUND_KEY_TYPE
holes.description: Type of one derived round key

template_code:
expand : [2]HALF_TYPE -> [ROUNDS]ROUND_KEY_TYPE
expand [kl, kr] =
    [ ((kl <<< i) # (kr <<< i)) @@ COMPRESSION_TABLE
    | i <- sums SHIFT_SCHEDULE ]

sums xs = ys
  where ys = [ x + y | x <- xs | y <- [0] # ys ]
usage_notes: Good for DES-style key schedules with cumulative rotation amounts.
Replace `#` plus `@@` with any other combine/select step that matches the target algorithm.
The `sums` helper is a reusable recurrence template for cumulative offsets.
retrieval_text: Template for a DES-style key schedule: cumulative shifts, rotate two halves with <<<, concatenate, then select/compress with a table.

title: Chunked S-box pipeline with per-chunk numbered dispatch
template_code:
applyBoxes : INPUT_TYPE -> OUTPUT_TYPE
applyBoxes x =
    join [ SINGLE_BOX_FUNC (n, chunk)
         | n     <- [1 .. BOX_COUNT]
         | chunk <- split x ]
usage_notes: Use when an input block is partitioned into fixed-size chunks and each chunk is transformed by a numbered lookup table.
For DES-style row/column indexing, define a second helper that slices outer and inner bits from each chunk.
If the target design uses one shared box instead of numbered boxes, remove the `n` parameter.
retrieval_text: Template for chunked substitution pipelines: split an input block into chunks, dispatch each chunk to a numbered box function, then join outputs.

title: Thread heterogeneous state through foldl with a tuple accumulator

holes.name: STATE_A
holes.description: First state component type, often an array or word

holes.name: STATE_B
holes.description: Second state component type, often a counter or flag

holes.name: STEP_INPUT
holes.description: Input item processed at each step

holes.name: STEP_BODY
holes.description: Update logic producing the next state

template_code:
step : (STATE_A, STATE_B) -> STEP_INPUT -> (STATE_A, STATE_B)
step (a, b) x = (a', b')
  where
    a' = STEP_BODY
    b' = STEP_BODY

result = foldl step initState inputs
usage_notes: Use when loop state mixes fields of different types.
Prefer a record instead of a tuple when there are three or more fields.
retrieval_text: Template for threading heterogeneous loop state through foldl using a tuple accumulator.

title: Value-level recursion for a runtime-bounded loop

holes.name: INDEX_TYPE
holes.description: Type of the runtime loop index or counter

template_code:
loop : INDEX_TYPE -> STATE_TYPE -> STATE_TYPE
loop i st =
  if DONE_TEST then st
  else loop NEXT_INDEX st'
    where
      st' = STATE_STEP
usage_notes: Use when the bound is a value such as len, remaining, or start.
Do not mix this with dynamically sized finite sequence generation in the same loop body.
retrieval_text: Template for expressing a runtime-bounded imperative loop as value-level recursion.

title: Pass the generator variable explicitly to a local helper

holes.name: RESULT_TYPE
holes.description: Element type of the output sequence

holes.name: BODY
holes.description: Element computation that depends on the generator variable

template_code:
out = [ elem i | i <- idxs ]
  where
    elem : IDX_TYPE -> RESULT_TYPE
    elem i = BODY
usage_notes: Use when a comprehension element needs intermediate names derived from the generator variable.
This avoids leaking generator-bound names into sibling `where` bindings.
retrieval_text: Template for keeping generator-dependent computations in scope by passing the generator variable explicitly to a helper.

title: Look up an authoritative constant table entry
template_code:
table : [N]WORD
table = TABLE_ENTRIES

lookup : INDEX_TYPE -> WORD
lookup idx = table @ INDEX_EXPR
usage_notes: Use when the spec already provides a trusted constant table such as zetas, S-boxes, or round constants.
Pair with a small index-normalization helper if the spec uses transformed indices.
retrieval_text: Template for direct lookup from an authoritative constant table.

title: Conservative unresolved skeleton for missing external primitives

holes.name: DEPENDENCY_NOTE
holes.description: Brief note naming the missing external primitive or type

template_code:
module MODULE_NAME where

// UNRESOLVED: DEPENDENCY_NOTE
FUNC_NAME : INPUT_TYPE -> OUTPUT_TYPE
FUNC_NAME x = undefined
usage_notes: Use only when the missing primitive is genuinely unavailable in the retrieved evidence.
This is preferable to pretending the main algorithm is fully implemented while referencing undefined names.
retrieval_text: Template for a conservative unresolved skeleton when key external primitives are missing.
